title: Geometry, Scale, Similarity, and Spatial Planning · Worked Case: Perimeter Area
summary: Create a scale plan for an accessible room layout with paths, clearances, paint, and flooring. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg perimeter area increment in an accessible scale floor plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose and justify perimeter or area models for borders, surfaces, and composite regions.
Classify angles and two- and three-dimensional shapes by defining properties rather than appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Perimeter Area evidence
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 8 m by 6 m. Calculate its area. Use the result to decide Perimeter Area for an accessible scale floor plan.
steps.why: The Perimeter Area result for an accessible scale floor plan is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.
